title: Foreign Object in the Eye
symptoms: Feeling like something is in the eye
immediate_steps: Wash hands before examining the eye
Have the person blink rapidly to naturally flush it out
Flush the eye gently with clean, warm water or saline solution from a cup, small glass, or gentle stream
Pull the upper eyelid over the lower eyelid to help brush the object out
If the object remains, cover both eyes (to prevent movement) and seek medical help
avoid: Do not use tweezers, cotton swabs, or anything hard to remove the object
Do not try to remove an object that is embedded in the eyeball

title: Chemical Splash in Eye
immediate_steps: Immediately flush the eye with a steady stream of lukewarm tap water for at least 15-20 minutes
Hold the eyelid open while flushing
Flush from the inner corner (near nose) outward
Remove contact lenses while flushing if possible
Call emergency services or Poison Control while flushing
avoid: Do not stop flushing to remove contacts (do it during)
Do not use any eye drops or chemical neutralizers
